summary: Dan Shapiro shares his excitement about ordering eCabinet Systems, a CAD software for cabinet design recommended by his woodworker neighbor. The software handles the full workflow from design to manufacturing: CAD modeling, rendered previews, wholesale hardware ordering, cut lists, and CNC mill-ready files. He plans to post a follow-up review once he's used it.
key_points: eCabinet Systems integrates the full cabinet-making pipeline from design through manufacturing preparation
The software was recommended by a semiprofessional woodworker neighbor, suggesting real-world credibility
Shapiro was already using TurboCAD 8.0 for general CAD work, indicating an active interest in digital fabrication tools
money_quotes: Evidently it lets you design your cabinets in a CAD environment, then shows you renderings of the finished cabinets, lets you order all the hardware wholesale, produces cutlists, and ultimately creates files you can send to have the wood milled for you.
key_insight: An early glimpse of Shapiro's longstanding interest in software that bridges digital design and physical fabrication — a thread that would eventually lead to Glowforge.
